message:
test(core): property-based tests for config, snapshot_engine, ingest modules

- proptest_config.rs: 63 tests covering LogFormat, SyncDirection, DistributedAuthMode,
  SnapshotSchedulingMode, PaneFilterRule/Config, PanePriorityConfig,
  CaptureBudgetConfig, RetentionTier, StorageConfig, SnapshotConfig serde/defaults
- proptest_snapshot_engine.rs: 37 tests covering SnapshotTrigger serde roundtrip
  for all 11 variants, SnapshotError Display/Debug, SnapshotResult,
  SnapshotSchedulingMode/Config defaults
- proptest_ingest.rs: 40 tests covering generate_pane_uuid, PaneFingerprint,
  ObservationDecision, PanePriorityOverride, DiscoveryDiff, PaneCursor,
  CapturedSegmentKind

Closes: wa-1ge9, wa-ypb7, wa-fzg3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1ez8","title":"Set up automated nightly rebase CI for WezTerm fork","description":"## Overview

WezTerm is updated nightly by its author. To maintain our feature branch, we need automated rebasing with conflict detection and alerting.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: wa-20fw (WezTerm fork with wa integration)

## CI Workflow Design

### GitHub Actions Workflow

\`\`\`.github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml\`\`\`
\`\`\`yaml
name: Rebase on Upstream WezTerm

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # Daily at 6 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:      # Manual trigger

jobs:
  rebase:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: feature/wa-integration
          token: \${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "wa-bot"
          git config user.email "wa-bot@example.com"
      
      - name: Add Upstream Remote
        run: |
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
          git fetch upstream main
      
      - name: Attempt Rebase
        id: rebase
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          git rebase upstream/main
          echo "rebase_status=\$?" >> \$GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Push if Successful
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          git push --force-with-lease origin feature/wa-integration
      
      - name: Create Conflict Issue
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: issues } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: 'rebase-conflict',
              state: 'open'
            });
            
            if (issues.length === 0) {
              await github.rest.issues.create({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                title: 'Rebase conflict with upstream WezTerm',
                body: \`The nightly rebase on upstream WezTerm failed due to conflicts.
                
                **Action Required**: Manual resolution needed.
                
                \\\`\\\`\\\`bash
                git fetch upstream main
                git rebase upstream/main
                # Resolve conflicts
                git rebase --continue
                git push --force-with-lease
                \\\`\\\`\\\`
                \`,
                labels: ['rebase-conflict', 'urgent']
              });
            }
      
      - name: Run Tests After Rebase
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          cargo build --features wa-integration
          cargo test --features wa-integration

  notify:
    needs: rebase
    if: failure()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send Notification
        run: |
          # Notify via email, Slack, Discord, etc.
          echo "Rebase failed - notification would be sent"
\`\`\`

### Handling Conflicts

When conflicts occur:
1. CI creates GitHub issue with "rebase-conflict" label
2. Human/agent reviews conflicting files
3. Usually conflicts are in files we modified (minimal set)
4. Resolve, test, push

### Minimizing Conflicts

To reduce conflict frequency:
1. Keep wa changes behind \`#[cfg(feature)]\` — no logic changes to upstream code
2. Use separate files where possible (mux/src/wa_events.rs)
3. Add wa code at end of functions, not middle
4. Avoid reformatting upstream code

## Alternative: Patch-Based Approach

Instead of maintaining a branch, maintain a patch series:

\`\`\`bash
# Generate patches
git format-patch upstream/main..feature/wa-integration -o patches/

# Apply patches to fresh clone
git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
cd wezterm
git am ../patches/*.patch
\`\`\`

Patches are more explicit about what we change, easier to review.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow created
- [ ] Nightly rebase runs automatically
- [ ] Conflicts create GitHub issues
- [ ] Notifications configured
- [ ] Post-rebase tests run
- [ ] Documentation for manual conflict resolution
- [ ] 30-day trial period: track conflict frequency

## Files to Create

- .github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml
- docs/maintaining-wezterm-fork.md

## References

- GitHub Actions: https://docs.github.com/en/actions
- Git rebase: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-rebase
- WezTerm release cadence: nightly builds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:53.398103664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.800522Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.800499Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1ez8","depends_on_id":"wa-20fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-1ez8","depends_on_id":"wa-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1hbj","title":"FTUI-05.1 Build base ftui app shell (tabs, layout, global status, view router)","description":"## Background\nAll view migrations depend on a stable shell runtime.\n\n## Deliverables\n- top-level ftui app scaffolding\n- view router + tab navigation surface\n- shared status/footer/error banner regions\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- shell hosts all target views\n- global nav semantics are defined and tested.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:07.004777535Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:41.224829807Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:41.224765858Z","close_reason":"FTUI-05.1 complete: WaModel implementing ftui::Model with init/update/view cycle, View enum with tab navigation, global keybindings, CellStyle abstraction, rendering helpers, run_tui entry point, 7 unit tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1hbj","depends_on_id":"wa-38vw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:07.017745Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1hbj","depends_on_id":"wa-3cso","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:20.038854745Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1hbj","depends_on_id":"wa-3kal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:29.52870044Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

 {"id":"wa-d248l","title":"Phase 3c: Rename feature flags vendored→frankenterm","description":"Rename feature flags in wa-core and wa Cargo.toml files:

## wa-core/Cargo.toml changes
- New: frankenterm = ["frankenterm-deps"]
- New: frankenterm-deps = ["dep:codec", "dep:config", "dep:mux", "dep:wezterm-term"]
- Changed: vendored = ["frankenterm"] (backward compat alias)
- Changed: vendored-wezterm = ["frankenterm-deps"] (backward compat alias)

## wa/Cargo.toml changes
- New: frankenterm = ["wa-core/frankenterm"]
- Changed: vendored = ["frankenterm"] (backward compat alias)

## Rationale
The name "vendored" no longer applies — we own the code. "frankenterm" signals the project's philosophy of combining best pieces from multiple sources. Backward compat aliases ensure existing CI/scripts/muscle-memory continue to work.

 {"id":"wa-d57z","title":"Design: prepare/commit UX + plan-hash binding semantics","description":"# Task: Design prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Specify the prepare/commit UX and the exact semantics of plan-hash binding.

## Requirements
- UX flows:
  - human CLI: prepare shows plan + hash, commit executes
  - robot: returns structured prepare result with remediation
  - MCP: same contract as robot
- Binding semantics:
  - approvals bind to:
    - workspace
    - plan_hash
    - action kind(s)
    - target pane_uuid (when applicable)
    - TTL
  - commit must refuse execution if:
    - plan hash mismatch
    - TTL expired
    - target pane identity changed
- Errors:
  - stable error codes and actionable remediation

## Acceptance Criteria
- The design prevents TOCTOU and confused-deputy approval mistakes.

 {"id":"wa-d8d1","title":"[EPIC] Audit log streaming + tailing","description":"## Background
Audit logs exist but are hard to consume in real time or integrate with external tooling.

## Goals
- Provide a streaming/tailing interface for audit logs
- Offer JSONL output for external ingestion (SIEM/log shipper)
- Ensure redaction and rate limiting

## Non-Goals
- External log shipping agent

## Considerations
- Streaming must not block writer threads
- Output must remain data-only (no ANSI)

## Success Criteria
- `wa audit tail` streams JSONL reliably
- Unit + e2e tests validate streaming and redaction","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:17:42.40508359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.317616-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T22:19:13.430195857Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-d8d1","depends_on_id":"wa-5em.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-d8d1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-d8d1","depends_on_id":"wa-05ca","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-d8d1","depends_on_id":"wa-9oy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-fwvx","title":"Explanation templates: reusable reason patterns for wa why and errors","description":"# Explanation Templates: Reusable reason patterns for wa why

## Purpose
Provide consistent, helpful explanations for common scenarios via template system.

## Implementation

### Template Structure
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ExplanationTemplate {
    pub id: &'static str,
    pub scenario: &'static str,
    pub brief: &'static str,
    pub detailed: &'static str,
    pub suggestions: Vec<&'static str>,
    pub see_also: Vec<&'static str>,
}
```

### Built-in Templates

#### Policy Denials
```rust
pub static DENY_ALT_SCREEN: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.alt_screen",
    scenario: "Send denied because alt-screen is active",
    brief: "Pane is in full-screen mode (vim, less, etc.)",
    detailed: r#"
The pane is currently displaying an alternate screen buffer, which typically
means a full-screen application like vim, less, htop, or similar is running.

Sending text while alt-screen is active could:
- Corrupt the application state
- Cause unintended keystrokes
- Interfere with user interaction

The safety policy blocks sends to alt-screen panes by default.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Exit the full-screen application first",
        "Use --force if you're certain this is safe",
        "Configure policy to allow specific alt-screen apps",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa policy", "wa status --pane <id>"],
};

pub static DENY_COMMAND_RUNNING: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.command_running",
    scenario: "Send denied because a command is running",
    brief: "Another command is currently executing in the pane",
    detailed: r#"
The pane has an active command running (detected via OSC 133 markers or
heuristics). Sending text while a command runs could:

- Interrupt the running command
- Queue input for later (confusing)
- Cause the shell to misinterpret input

wa waits for command completion before sending unless overridden.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Wait for the current command to finish",
        "Use Ctrl-C to cancel the running command first",
        "Use --wait-for to send after a specific pattern",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa status", "wa send --wait-for"],
};

pub static DENY_RECENT_GAP: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.recent_gap",
    scenario: "Send denied due to recent output gap",
    brief: "Pane had no output recently, possibly waiting for input",
    detailed: r#"
wa detected a gap in pane output that suggests the pane might be:
- Waiting for user input at a prompt
- Displaying a confirmation dialog
- In an unknown state

The policy requires a prompt marker (OSC 133) or manual confirmation.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Check the pane manually to see its state",
        "Use --force if you've verified the pane is ready",
        "Enable OSC 133 support in your shell for better detection",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa capabilities --pane <id>"],
};
```

#### Workflow Explanations
```rust
pub static WORKFLOW_USAGE_LIMIT: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "workflow.usage_limit",
    scenario: "Why handle_usage_limits workflow was triggered",
    brief: "Codex hit its daily token usage limit",
    detailed: r#"
The Codex agent reported it has reached its usage limit. This typically
happens when:

- Daily token quota exceeded
- Account-level rate limiting triggered

The handle_usage_limits workflow will:
1. Gracefully exit the current Codex session
2. Parse the session summary for resume ID
3. Select an alternate OpenAI account
4. Complete device auth flow
5. Resume the session with new credentials
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Let the workflow complete automatically",
        "Check account status with: caut status",
        "Configure account pool in wa.toml",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa workflow status", "caut"],
};
```

### Template Registry
```rust
lazy_static! {
    pub static ref EXPLANATION_TEMPLATES: HashMap<&'static str, &'static ExplanationTemplate> = {
        let mut m = HashMap::new();
        m.insert("deny.alt_screen", &DENY_ALT_SCREEN);
        m.insert("deny.command_running", &DENY_COMMAND_RUNNING);
        m.insert("deny.recent_gap", &DENY_RECENT_GAP);
        m.insert("workflow.usage_limit", &WORKFLOW_USAGE_LIMIT);
        // ... more templates
        m
    };
}

pub fn get_explanation(id: &str) -> Option<&'static ExplanationTemplate> {
    EXPLANATION_TEMPLATES.get(id).copied()
}
```

### Template Interpolation
```rust
pub fn render_explanation(
    template: &ExplanationTemplate,
    context: &HashMap<String, String>,
) -> String {
    let mut output = template.detailed.to_string();
    for (key, value) in context {
        output = output.replace(&format!("{{{}}}", key), value);
    }
    output
}
```

## Testing
- All templates have valid structure
- Interpolation works correctly
- No broken cross-references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Templates for all common denial reasons
- [ ] Templates for all workflow triggers
- [ ] wa why uses templates consistently
- [ ] Templates documented in help

 {"id":"wa-fykv","title":"FTUI-03.2.a Route in-process logs through single output sink","description":"## Background
In-process output must obey one-writer constraints to keep rendering deterministic and avoid output corruption.

## Deliverables
- inventory and removal plan for direct stdout/stderr writes in UI-active paths
- unified output sink routing contract for logs/events/status lines
- verification checks to prove no bypass writes remain

## Acceptance Criteria
- all targeted paths route through the sanctioned sink while UI is active
- checks detect and fail on new bypass writes

 {"id":"wa-g08v","title":"Quick-fix tests: template interpolation, error remediation coverage, UX validation","description":"
# Quick-Fix Testing Suite

## Purpose
Ensure all suggestions are accurate, copy-pasteable, and helpful.

## Test Categories

### 1. Template Interpolation Tests
- {pane} substitution works
- {event_id} substitution works
- Invalid templates fail gracefully

### 2. Error Remediation Coverage
- Every WaError variant has remediation
- Remediations are non-empty
- Commands are syntactically valid

### 3. UX Validation Tests
- Commands can be copy-pasted (no smart quotes, etc.)
- Commands are actually runnable
- Suggestions match actual system state

### 4. Context-Aware Tests
- Typo detection finds correct suggestions
- Available resources are accurate
- Platform detection is correct

### 5. Snapshot Tests
- Error message format stability
- Suggestion format stability

## Test Fixtures
- Error scenarios with known remediations
- Event scenarios with known suggestions
- Platform-specific command sets

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% of error types have remediation tests
- [ ] 100% of rule types have suggestion tests
- [ ] Snapshot tests for output stability
- [ ] Copy-paste validation (no encoding issues)

## Testing
- Unit tests for template rendering and suggested_action interpolation.
- Integration tests: fixture errors/events produce actionable suggestions.
- E2E: extend an events/error scenario to assert suggestions and rule-id logs.

 {"id":"wa-g2l","title":"[EPIC] Phase 4: Polish & Integration","description":"# Phase 4: Polish & Integration

## Overview
This epic focuses on user experience, external integrations, and robustness. After Phase 3, wa works - now we make it delightful to use and integrate with the broader ecosystem.

## Strategic Importance
Phase 4 transforms wa from "working prototype" to "production-ready tool":
- MCP server enables AI agents to control wa
- rich_rust output makes CLI pleasant for humans
- Setup automation reduces friction for new users
- CASS integration enables session archaeology
- Comprehensive testing ensures reliability

## Key Components

### 1. MCP Server with fastmcp_rust
Tools:
- wa.state - Get current pane states
- wa.get_text - Get text from pane
- wa.send - Send text to pane
- wa.wait_for - Wait for pattern
- wa.search - FTS search
- wa.events - Get events
- wa.workflow_run - Execute workflow
- wa.accounts - List accounts
- wa.accounts_refresh - Refresh account usage
- wa.rules_list - List detection rules
- wa.rules_test - Test pattern matching

Resources:
- wa://panes - Current pane registry
- wa://events - Event feed
- wa://accounts - Account status
- wa://workflows - Available workflows
- wa://rules - Pattern rules

### 2. rich_rust CLI Output
- render_pane_table() - Tables for pane listings
- render_event_panel() - Panels for events with severity colors
- Progress indicators for long operations
- Consistent styling across all commands

### 3. Setup Automation

#### SSH Config Parser
- Parse ~/.ssh/config
- Extract hosts, hostnames, users, ports, identity files
- Filter out wildcards

#### Automated Remote Setup
1. Check if wezterm is installed
2. Install WezTerm if needed (apt/dnf)
3. Create systemd service for wezterm-mux-server
4. Enable lingering for service persistence
5. Optionally install wa binary
6. Verify service is running

#### WezTerm Config Generator
- Generate ssh_domains with WezTerm multiplexing
- Domain-specific colors (optional)
- wa integration hooks (user-var-changed forwarding)
- Idempotent patching (WA-BEGIN/WA-END markers)

### 4. CASS Integration (coding_agent_session_search)
- find_session(session_id) - Correlate wa session with cass data
- search_sessions(path, agent) - Find sessions in a directory
- Session archaeology for context recovery
- Token usage correlation

### 5. Comprehensive Testing

#### Unit Tests
- Delta extraction correctness (overlap cases, wrap cases)
- Pattern rules per agent (positive + negative fixtures)
- Workflow step guards (prompt detection, alt-screen checks)
- DB schema migration tests
- Policy engine capability checks

#### Integration Tests
- Simulate wezterm cli outputs via fixtures
- Run workflow engine against fake panes
- Test StorageHandle async operations

#### Golden Corpus Regression Tests
Directory structure:
\`\`\`
tests/corpus/
├── codex/
│   ├── usage_limit_v1.txt
│   ├── usage_limit_v1.expect.json
├── claude_code/
│   ├── session_end.txt
│   └── session_end.expect.json
└── gaps/
    ├── scrollback_truncation.txt
    └── scrollback_truncation.expect.json
\`\`\`

#### Property-Based Tests (proptest)
- Segment sequence monotonicity
- FTS finds inserted text
- Delta extraction completeness

#### Performance Budgets (Criterion)
- pattern_detection_typical: p50 < 1ms, p99 < 5ms
- fts_query_common: p50 < 10ms, p99 < 50ms
- CI enforcement with baseline comparison

## Success Criteria
- [ ] MCP server exposes all robot mode functionality
- [ ] CLI output is beautiful with rich_rust
- [ ] \`wa setup\` configures local and remote hosts
- [ ] CASS integration works for session correlation
- [ ] Test coverage > 70% overall
- [ ] All performance budgets met
- [ ] Documentation sufficient for contributors

## Dependencies
- Depends on Phase 1-3 (all core functionality)

## Configuration
At this point, wa.toml should be fully functional:

\`\`\`toml
[general]
log_level = "info"
data_dir = "~/.local/share/wa"

[ingest]
poll_interval_ms = 200
gap_detection = true

[storage]
db_path = "~/.local/share/wa/wa.db"
retention_days = 30

[patterns]
packs = ["builtin:core", "builtin:codex", "builtin:claude_code"]

[workflows]
enabled = ["handle_compaction", "handle_usage_limits"]
max_concurrent = 3

[safety]
rate_limit_per_pane = 30
require_prompt_active = true

[metrics]
enabled = false
bind = "127.0.0.1:9464"
\`\`\`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:48:07.901261212Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:10.578560397Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:10.578560397Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of wa-nu4.3 (Phase 4: Polish & Integration). wa-nu4.3 has correct priority (P1) and dependency chain."}

 {"id":"wa-upg.9.6","title":"E2E: triage with multiple simultaneous issues (artifacts + suggested actions)","description":"# Task: E2E triage multi-issue scenario

## Goal
Validate that triage prioritization works in realistic multi-symptom situations.

## Scenario
Create multiple issues:
- a recent crash bundle
- a repeated event with escalation
- a stuck workflow
- a health warning (index lag)

Run `wa triage` and assert ordering and suggested actions.

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - triage JSON output
  - logs for each simulated issue

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates operator usefulness (clear ordering and actions).

 {"id":"wa-upg.9.7","title":"Docs: operator playbook (using triage + why + reproduce)","description":"# Task: Document operator playbook

## Goal
Provide a pragmatic operator guide for keeping wa healthy.

## Requirements
- Document common flows:
  - triage → why → fix
  - triage → reproduce → file issue
  - triage → mute/noise control
- Provide copy-paste examples.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new user can recover from common incidents using only the playbook.

 {"id":"wa-uyve","title":"[EPIC] Saved searches + scheduled alerts","description":"## Background
Operators repeatedly run the same search/event filters to track specific agent states. Today this is manual and easy to forget.

## Goals
- Persist named searches (query + filters + scope) for reuse
- Allow scheduled runs that emit notifications when results appear
- Integrate with CLI/TUI/web dashboards without duplicating search logic

## Non-Goals
- New query language (reuse existing FTS syntax)
- External alert delivery (handled in notification channels epic)

## Considerations
- Saved search must be safe to execute (no side effects)
- Scheduling should be rate-limited and observable
- Outputs must be redacted per safety config

## Success Criteria
- Users can create/list/run/delete saved searches
- Scheduled alerts fire with clear context and can be disabled
- Full unit + e2e test coverage with verbose logs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:03.835297223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.253264-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:29.507396585Z","close_reason":"All child beads completed; closing stale-open epic to keep active plan set non-duplicative."}

 {"id":"wa-ye8","title":"Enriched error messages: every error includes actionable remediation","description":"# Task: Enriched Error Messages

## Goal
Transform all wa error messages to include **actionable remediation steps**. Errors become guides, not dead ends.

## Why This Matters
Cryptic errors waste user time and create frustration:
- "Error: DB locked" → What do I do?
- "Error: Pane not found" → Which panes exist?
- "Error: Send denied" → Why? How do I fix it?

Enriched errors answer the "now what?" question immediately.

## Error Message Standard
Every error must include:
1. **What happened**: Clear description
2. **Why it happened**: Context/cause (when knowable)
3. **How to fix it**: Specific, copy-pasteable commands (prefer safe commands)
4. **Learn more**: Link to docs or `wa why` command

## Examples

### Before
```
Error: DB locked
```

### After
```
Error: Cannot start watcher - workspace lock is held

Another wa process may already be running.

To fix:
  - See who is running it: wa doctor
  - Stop the watcher safely: wa stop
  - If the lock appears stale, follow wa doctor's instructions (do not guess)

Learn more:
  - wa why (for decision/audit explanations)
  - wa doctor (for environment + workspace paths)
```

### Before
```
Error: Pane 99 not found
```

### After
```
Error: Pane 99 not found

Available panes:
  1: codex @ /home/user/project
  3: claude @ /home/user/other
  7: gemini @ /home/user/third

Did you mean: wa send --pane 9 "hello" (closest match)

To list all panes: wa status
```

### Before
```
Error: Send denied
```

### After
```
Error: Send denied - pane is in AltScreen mode

Pane 3 (claude_code) is currently running a full-screen application.
Sending input now could corrupt the application state.

To fix:
  - Wait for the application to exit
  - Check pane state: wa status --pane 3

To understand:
  - wa why denied --pane 3

To proceed if you truly must override:
  - use the allow-once approval flow (wa will provide a short code)
  - run: wa approve <code>
  - retry the original action
```

## Implementation

### 1. Error Type with Remediation
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum WaError {
    #[error("Cannot start watcher - workspace lock is held")]
    WorkspaceLockHeld {
        lock_path: PathBuf,
        remediation: Vec<Remediation>,
    },

    #[error("Pane {pane_id} not found")]
    PaneNotFound {
        pane_id: PaneId,
        available_panes: Vec<PaneInfo>,
        closest_match: Option<PaneId>,
        remediation: Vec<Remediation>,
    },
    // ... more variants
}

pub enum Remediation {
    Command { description: &'static str, command: String },
    Url { description: &'static str, url: &'static str },
    Hint { text: String },
}
```

### 2. Error Formatting
```rust
impl WaError {
    pub fn display_rich(&self) -> String {
        let mut output = String::new();
        output.push_str(&format!("Error: {}\n\n", self));

        if let Some(context) = self.context() {
            output.push_str(&format!("{}\n\n", context));
        }

        output.push_str("To fix:\n");
        for remedy in self.remediation() {
            output.push_str(&format!("  - {}\n", remedy));
        }

        output
    }
}
```

### 3. Context-Aware Remediation
Use runtime context to generate specific commands:
- include actual paths, pane IDs, timestamps
- detect platform for OS-specific commands
- show closest matches for typos

### 4. Integration Points
- CLI error handler uses `display_rich()`
- Robot mode errors include remediation in JSON
- MCP errors include remediation hints

## Testing
- Unit tests: Each error type has valid remediation
- Integration tests: Errors include context-specific values
- Snapshot tests: Error messages match expected format
- UX tests: Remediation steps actually work

## Acceptance Criteria
- All error messages include "To fix:" with specific steps
- Context-specific values (paths, IDs) are included
- Remediation commands can be copy-pasted directly
- Error messages are consistent in format
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:27.969274665Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:26:28.706917403Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T04:26:28.706826712Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete: Every error type (WaError, WeztermError, StorageError, PatternError, WorkflowError, ConfigError) has remediation() method. All acceptance criteria met: 'To fix' with specific steps, context-specific values, copy-pasteable commands, consistent format. Previously addressed by wa-bnm.2."}
 {"id":"wa-yi0l","title":"Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs","description":"# Task: Implement MCP tools (full set)

## Goal
Implement the full MCP tool set defined in `wa-nu4.3.1.1` by delegating to internal/robot APIs.

## Principle
**One source of truth**: MCP handlers must call the same internal functions used by CLI/robot mode.
No duplicated parsing/validation logic.

## Tool implementation checklist
For each tool:
- Validate inputs (types, required fields)
- Call internal function
- Map result into MCP response schema
- Ensure errors are stable (`WA-MCP-...`)

Tools:
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- **`wa.reservations`**
- **`wa.reserve`**
- **`wa.release`**

## Safety
- `wa.send`, `wa.workflow_run`, and `wa.reserve` must enforce PolicyEngine.
- Consider default-deny for any new tool that could mutate state.

## Testing
- Covered by MCP tests (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`):
  - schema stability
  - parity vs robot outputs
  - policy-gated tools denied when appropriate

## Deliverables
- Tool registry wiring for all tools.
- Consistent error codes and `ok/data/error` envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each MCP tool can be invoked successfully with a trivial request.
- Tools that would act on panes are rejected when policy disallows.


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ bd-4vx.7: [EPIC] Robot mode CLI (stable JSON envelope + core commands) ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.1.5: Workflow runner: consume Detection events, select workflow, execute with policy gates ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.5: [EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth) ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.1.9: [Robot] `wa robot workflow` (run workflow by name, stable result schema) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.2.1.4: Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ bd-nu4.3.1.5: MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs ● P2
  ← ○ bd-nu4.3.1.6: MCP audit integration: record each tool call decision/outcome (redacted) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-ytyo","title":"[EPIC] Phase 3: Full agent support — complete packs + account rotation","description":"# Phase 3 — Full Agent Support

## Objective
Expand from “works for the main path” to “works broadly”:
- Complete patterns and workflows for **Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini**.
- Harden account rotation logic and integrate usage tracking as a first-class system.

## Why this phase exists
Agent CLIs change output formats over time; partial coverage creates fragility. This phase invests in:
- more complete detection packs
- richer structured extraction
- regression fixtures (“golden corpus”)
- workflow variants per agent

## Deliverables
- Rule packs:
  - `core.codex`: full coverage for usage warnings/reached/session summaries/auth prompts.
  - `core.claude_code`: compaction + banner + usage signals (as discovered in field).
  - `core.gemini`: usage reached + session summary + model changes + resume mechanics.

- Workflows:
  - `handle_usage_limits` implemented for all three agents (behavior may differ).
  - `handle_session_end` (capture + store structured session summaries).
  - `handle_auth_required` (centralize login flows).

- Accounts:
  - `caut` becomes the source of truth for usage; wa stores a historical mirror.
  - Selection policy (percent remaining + LRU tie-break).

## Acceptance criteria
- Primary detections for all three agents are covered by unit tests and golden corpus fixtures.
- At least one real-world end-to-end run exists for each agent’s usage-limit handling path (or documented safe fallback if truly impossible to automate).



## Success Criteria
- Rule packs and workflows expand to support all target agents (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini) with consistent behavior.
- Account rotation and agent-specific workflows are covered by regression tests and golden corpora.
- User-facing surfaces (robot/MCP/human CLI) remain stable as capabilities expand.


## Testing
- Golden corpus coverage:
  - Every new rule added in this phase must land with fixtures (positive + near-miss negative).
  - Corpus tests must fail loudly on drift and point to the exact rule + capture that changed.

- Cross-agent workflow regression tests:
  - Use shared workflow engine tests plus per-agent fixtures to validate semantics remain consistent (idempotency, resume, safe failure).

- E2E per-agent reality checks:
  - For each agent (Codex/Claude/Gemini), capture at least one real transcript and ensure it’s represented in fixtures.
  - If true automation is not possible (MFA), require an E2E “fails safe” scenario that still produces artifacts and instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-yy9z","title":"[Human command] wa stop (stop watcher in workspace safely)","description":"# Task: Human command wa stop

## Goal
Give users a safe, ergonomic way to stop a running `wa watch` for a given workspace.

Many users will run `wa watch` in the background (or via terminal multiplexers). Telling them to manually find and kill a PID is user-hostile.

## Behavior (v0)
- `wa stop`
  - resolves the workspace (flag/env/default)
  - reads the watch lock metadata (pid/started_at/host/version)
  - sends SIGTERM to the watcher pid (preferred) and waits for the lock to be released
  - prints a clear summary:
    - which workspace
    - which pid
    - whether shutdown was graceful

- `wa stop --force`
  - if graceful shutdown times out, escalates to SIGKILL (only with explicit `--force`)

## Safety
- Never kills an arbitrary PID:
  - only act if the lock metadata matches the workspace lock
  - require the process to be owned by the same user (best-effort check)

## UX
- TTY: rich panel output.
- Non-TTY: stable JSON.

## Testing
- Integration test:
  - start a watcher-like process that holds the lock
  - run `wa stop`
  - assert lock is released and process exits

- E2E:
  - `wa-4vx.10.21` validates stop+restart behavior and captures artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa stop` reliably stops a watcher started in the same workspace.
- `wa stop` errors are actionable when no watcher is running.
